description: Refactor App.tsx to extract the monolithic notification handler into a dedicated module and fix the ref-sync effect's missing dependency array.

userStories.title: Extract notification handler from App.tsx useEffect
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the server notification handling logic extracted from App.tsx into a separate module so that the main component is easier to read and the notification logic can be tested independently.
acceptanceCriteria: The handleNotification function logic (confirmation.request, tab.stateChanged, tool.invocationStart, tool.invocationEnd) is moved out of App.tsx
App.tsx useEffect is shorter and delegates to the extracted module
The side panel behavior is unchanged — confirmations, tab state updates, and tool invocation indicators still work
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:69-220, a single useEffect contains ~150 lines: handleNotification (~70 lines at lines 79-148) plus the listener function (~60 lines at lines 150-216). The handleNotification function handles 4 notification types (confirmation.request, tab.stateChanged, tool.invocationStart, tool.invocationEnd) and calls React state setters. Extract this into a custom hook or a standalone handler function. The handler needs access to: setPlugins, setActiveTools, setPendingConfirmations, pendingTabStates ref, and constants like VALID_PLUGIN_NAME and validTabStates. A clean approach: create a useServerNotifications hook that takes the state setters as params and returns a handleNotification function. Alternatively, move handleNotification to module scope and pass setters as arguments. Either approach is acceptable — pick whichever produces cleaner code.

userStories.title: Add explicit dependency array to ref-sync effect in App.tsx
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ref-sync effect in App.tsx to have an explicit dependency array so that the code communicates its intent clearly.
acceptanceCriteria: The useEffect at (approximately) line 41-45 of App.tsx that syncs connectedRef, loadingRef, and pluginsRef has the dependency array [connected, loading, plugins]
The behavior is unchanged — refs still correctly reflect current state for the sp:getState handler
userStories.notes: In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx:41-45, the effect has no dependency array and runs on every render. Adding [connected, loading, plugins] is semantically identical — the refs only need updating when these values change. This is a one-line change: add the dependency array. The refs are read by the sp:getState message handler (line 155-168) which needs the latest values to respond to E2E test queries.
